expectations.text: Decided the marketing skill applies and used it
expectations.evidence: Response opens with 'Assuming audience: B2B SaaS founders...' and produces structured cold email copy, consistent with skill execution.

expectations.text: Subject line is present and under ~50 characters
expectations.evidence: Subject: 'The $[X] you're losing before you see it leave' is approximately 47 characters.

expectations.text: Includes preview/preheader text complementary to the subject
expectations.evidence: Preview text: 'Most churn is predictable 30 days before it happens.' complements the subject line.

expectations.text: Opening is relevance-first (no generic throat-clearing like 'I hope this finds you well')
expectations.evidence: Email opens immediately with 'The average SaaS loses 5–7% of MRR to churn every month.' — no generic greeting.

expectations.text: Body is one idea and under ~120 words
expectations.evidence: The body focuses solely on early churn signals and is approximately 90 words.

expectations.text: Has exactly one low-friction CTA (yes/no or permission ask), not a 30-minute meeting demand
expectations.evidence: 'Worth a 15-minute look? [Book a slot here →]' is a meeting booking request, not a yes/no or permission ask.

expectations.text: States assumptions inline rather than blocking with questions (scoped/low-stakes task)
expectations.evidence: Response opens: 'Assuming audience: B2B SaaS founders running a product with monthly subscribers; adjust if wrong.'
